title: columella corniculata
intro: Columella corniculata, also known as wild comfrey, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family, native to Europe and parts of Asia. The plant contains allantoin, a compound with medicinal properties that can help to promote skin regeneration, wound healing, and tissue repair. The health benefits of Columella corniculata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support bone health, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Columella corniculata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used topically to accelerate wound healing and reduce the appearance of scars.
